summary.intent: Implement story 5-2: build a full AI Metrics Dashboard page with 5 section components, utility functions, unit tests, and integrate Chart.js into an Astro project.
summary.outcome: Story 5-2 fully implemented and marked for review: 20 metrics across 5 section components, 36 new unit tests (148 total passing), 0 lint errors, and a confirmed intact build guard.

repo: The project uses a BMAD workflow with sprint-status.yaml to track story state (ready-for-dev → in-progress → review).
Story implementation artifacts live in _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/ and must be updated with task completion, file lists, and change logs.
The Astro build has a deliberate build guard (from story 5-1) that fires an expected error when checkpoints-cache.json is absent — this is correct behavior per AC2, not a bug.
Data is loaded via fs.readFileSync from checkpoints-cache.json at build time; a cache-missing notice is shown when the file is absent.
eslint.config.js used eslint-plugin-astro but lacked @typescript-eslint/parser, causing TypeScript-in-Astro lint failures across the whole codebase (pre-existing issue, not introduced by this story).

code.finding: Required adding @typescript-eslint/parser as the languageOptions.parser for Astro files to resolve TypeScript lint errors in .astro frontmatter blocks.
Contains DARK_CHART_OPTIONS constant and 6 compute functions for transforming checkpoint data into Chart.js datasets; also re-exports relevant types.
Had an unused variable that required a follow-up edit immediately after initial write.

workflow: All 7 implementation tasks (layout, page, 5 components, utils, tests) were written in two parallel batches, significantly reducing sequential overhead.
Running lint before tests would have caught the TypeScript parser config issue earlier in the cycle.
The tip at story completion explicitly recommends using a different LLM for code review to avoid self-review bias — a deliberate BMAD workflow practice.

friction: The eslint.config.js did not have @typescript-eslint/parser configured for Astro files, requiring investigation of the eslint-plugin-astro internals (multiple bash probes into node_modules) before identifying the fix.
chart.js was not pre-installed, requiring an npm install step before implementation could begin.
AiTemporalSection.astro had an unused variable bug that required an immediate follow-up edit after initial write.
open_items: Code review of story 5-2 is pending — recommended to be done by a different LLM than the one that implemented it.
The pre-existing lint misconfiguration (missing TypeScript parser for Astro) was fixed as part of this story but was not the original scope — worth noting as incidental debt resolved.
